output: SPEC.md created for YardVision. It selected industrial-mission-control, terminal-palantir, and cinematic-frame-sequence under a registry heading. It includes an Asset Plan, desktop hero, mobile hero, poster, reduced-motion, and scroll checkpoints. The spec also mentions generic SaaS as something to avoid, which exposes a brittle wording problem for this eval.

The observed spec and manifest mention assets/asset-manifest.json, poster frame, reduced-motion still, HTML/CSS overlays, keep readable text out of generated media, and asset load checks. The observed baseline did not operationalize separate desktop frames or mobile frames; it fell back to code-native SVG scenes and future video prompts.

Observed Pi/Kimi output eventually produced index.html, styles.css, app.js, SPEC.md, and asset-manifest.json after several phase-repair prompts. It timed out before a clean handoff, screenshot judgment, or external self-review. Desktop and mobile screenshots were captured by Farplane afterward.

gold reference: https://terminal-industries.com/ screenshots. Terminal gap: first viewport uses a real physical operation with cinematic asset quality, deliberate mobile crop, and strong truck scale. The generated page has a physical operation motif but too much empty darkness, weak SVG media, mobile nav overflow, and no true frame-sequence asset pipeline. skill improvement hypothesis: require spec-first phase splitting, generated media prompts, asset manifests, and visual-review checkpoints before claiming Terminal-quality output.
